data.title: Implementation-plan citation annotation with local-first reference library merging
data.description:
## Context

With Confluence integration (e471-dda3) and UI/UX design corpus (0b7c-d811) in place, /dso:implementation-plan still generates tasks without referencing org-specific guidance stored in Confluence or curated design knowledge from the local corpus. This epic closes that gap by adding `ref-library-loader.sh` — a pipeline that queries the local corpus first, supplements with configured URL references, merges the two sets, applies LLM relevance judgment, and annotates each generated task with cited references in one of two formats. Single user outcome: /dso:implementation-plan tasks cite relevant reference sources.

## Success Criteria

- For each task generated by /dso:implementation-plan, ref-library-loader.sh annotates the task with up to 6 relevant reference citations drawn from the merged corpus+URL reference set (headroom from merged-set cap of 20 allows LLM relevance judgment to select top 6); tasks with no relevant matches (merged set empty after LLM judgment excludes all candidates) are generated without citations and no error is raised. The "6" constant is documented in ref-library-loader.sh as `MAX_CITATIONS_PER_TASK=6`.
- Two citation formats supported and visually distinguishable in task output: URL-sourced `> Reference: [Doc Title](url) — [one-sentence excerpt]` and corpus-sourced `> Reference: [Doc Title] (dso:ui-reference:<entry-id>) — [one-sentence excerpt]`.
- ref-library-loader.sh pipeline (per task): (a) query local UI/UX corpus via `dso ref-query --top-n=8` using task title+description with 30s subprocess timeout and explicit PID tracking (no zombie processes); (b) apply topic-tag substring filter to configured URL references, fetch via `confluence-fetch.sh`, cap post-filter set at 12 with consistent budget accounting (failed fetches excluded, retry quota re-filled from next candidate to maintain target budget); (c) merge both sets with local-first precedence — corpus entries preserved; URL slots expand to fill unused corpus budget ONLY when corpus returns fewer than 8 AND merged-set cap of 20 has remaining capacity (explicit slot-reallocation documented); (d) LLM relevance judgment per-entry with UTF-8-safe 200-character excerpt truncation at character boundary, concurrent-invocation cap of 3; (e) select top 6 entries by LLM relevance score for citation annotation.
- Topic-tag filter with no configured tags defaults to match-none semantics (URL references with empty tags field excluded from filter output) — documented default. URL references with explicit tags use substring matching against task title and description.
- Runtime guards: (a) `command -v dso ref-query` at startup; absent → stderr warning, URL-only mode; (b) `command -v confluence-fetch.sh` at startup; absent → stderr warning, corpus-only mode; (c) missing `confluence.base_url` key → configuration error at startup, URL fetch path aborts, corpus-only mode with clear stderr error; (d) BOTH unavailable → stderr warning, tasks generated without citations, no error.
- Integration test suite at tests/integration/test-ref-library-loader-integration.sh validates: (a) corpus-only mode returns corpus-sourced citations; (b) URL-only mode returns URL-sourced citations; (c) merge mode returns both formats with verified local-first precedence; (d) sparse corpus (ref-query returns 3 entries) reallocates up to 5 URL slots without crowd-out; (e) both-unavailable mode generates tasks without citations and no error; (f) UTF-8 multibyte characters in excerpts never split mid-codepoint.
- Validation signal (executable within sprint): Before/after comparison on exactly 3 representative implementation-plan tasks drawn from recent org work. Baseline (ref-library-loader inactive) vs. treatment (ref-library-loader active). Pass threshold: ALL 3 treatment tasks must cite at least one Confluence or corpus source (URL-sourced or corpus-sourced format) that is ABSENT from the corresponding baseline task's generated output, verified by string comparison of the full annotated vs. unannotated task body (citation lines grep'd on prefix `> Reference:`). Any of the 3 comparisons failing this check fails the validation signal and blocks sprint closure.

## Sprint Scope Rationale

This epic delivers a single pipeline with one user outcome — /dso:implementation-plan tasks cite relevant reference sources. The three internal steps (corpus query, URL fetch, merge-and-judge) are tightly coupled: the merge logic depends on outputs from both sources; LLM relevance judgment operates on the merged set; the slot-reallocation rule for sparse corpus only has meaning when both paths coexist. Splitting into corpus-only Milestone A and URL-merge Milestone B would ship an incomplete pipeline whose primary value (merged citations with local-first precedence) is unavailable until Milestone B ships. YAGNI: additional decomposition creates sequencing overhead without standalone user value in Milestone A.

## Dependencies

- e471-dda3 (BLOCKING): requires confluence-fetch.sh infrastructure for URL fetch path
- 0b7c-d811 (BLOCKING): requires dso ref-query CLI + committed _index.yaml for corpus query path
- Sprint ordering: ships after both parents merge; runtime guards permit partial deployment but full value requires both parents

## Approach

ref-library-loader.sh invoked as a post-rendering step in /dso:implementation-plan task generation — receives fully-rendered task title+description as input (no template variables). Pipeline executes per task. Corpus query via dso ref-query with 30s timeout + explicit PID tracking. URL fetch via confluence-fetch.sh from e471-dda3. Merge step implements explicit slot-reallocation for local-first precedence with sparse-corpus fallback. LLM relevance judgment per-entry (not batched) with concurrent-invocation cap of 3 to balance throughput and rate limits.

## Research Findings

Inherited from parent epics e471-dda3 (Confluence CQL API prior art, Basic Auth patterns, redirect-based credential exfiltration mitigation) and 0b7c-d811 (stdlib BM25 retrieval, corpus schema, _index.yaml format). Additional epic-specific findings: per-entry LLM relevance judgment (vs. batched) preferred at this scale to avoid context-length failures while keeping per-task wall time bounded via concurrent-invocation cap of 3 (empirical sweet spot for rate-limit-bound LLM clients).

## Scenario Analysis

- dso ref-query subprocess hangs past 30s timeout leaving zombie process (high, runtime): 30s timeout alone insufficient — must track child PID and kill on timeout; N tasks → N zombies without PID tracking.
- confluence-fetch.sh fetch failures cause inconsistent budget accounting across tasks in the same plan (high, runtime): failed fetches must not count against 12-entry budget; retry quota refills from next candidate.
- CI/CD pipeline runs ref-library-loader.sh without corpus present, producing different output than developer local runs (high, deployment): `command -v dso ref-query` guard passes if `dso` exists but subcommand missing; check subcommand registration explicitly.
- Missing dso-config.conf key for Confluence base URL causes confluence-fetch.sh to silently fetch from wrong endpoint (high, configuration): ref-library-loader must surface URL-fetch-skipped diagnostic even when corpus path succeeds.
- LLM relevance judgment token budget overflow when 20 entries with 200-char excerpts are passed in a single prompt (high, runtime): per-entry judgment (not batched) with concurrent-invocation cap of 3 avoids both context-length and rate-limit failures.
- 200-character excerpt truncation splits multibyte UTF-8 characters, corrupting citation output (medium, runtime): truncate at character boundary (not byte offset); integration test (f) verifies.
- Topic-tag filter with no configured tags matches all URL references, exhausting the 12-entry budget on irrelevant results (medium, configuration): default to match-none when tags empty; documented default.
- Max-20 merge cap is not split proportionally, allowing URL references to crowd out corpus results (medium, configuration): explicit slot-reallocation rule — URL slots expand only when corpus returns fewer than 8 AND merge-cap headroom exists.

### Planning Intelligence Log

- **Web research (Step 2.6)**: inherited from parent epics
  - Bright-line conditions that fired in parents: external integration (Confluence CQL, dso ref-query), novel pattern (local-first merge with slot reallocation)
- **Scenario analysis (Step 2.75)**: triggered
  - Scenarios surviving blue team filter: 8
- **Practitioner-requested additional cycles**: Agent Clarity re-review 1 time (fix SC1 K definition + SC7 pass threshold); Scope re-review 1 time (YAGNI rationale against further split)
- **Follow-on scrutiny (Step 0)**: not triggered — this epic is itself a follow-on from e471-dda3; depth 0
- **Feasibility resolution (Step 2.5)**: not triggered
- **LLM-instruction signal (Step 5)**: triggered — keyword: skill file modifications (implementation-plan SKILL.md post-rendering hook for ref-library-loader.sh invocation)
- **Scale context (Step 0)**: small scale (default) — per-implementation-plan-task (moderate number of plans per day, 5-20 tasks per plan); not volume-sensitive at current scale
